description: Evidence-informed coping skills organized by target emotion

strategies.name: 5-4-3-2-1 grounding
strategies.instructions: Name 5 things you can see. 4 things you can touch. 3 things you can hear. 2 things you can smell. 1 thing you can taste. Say them out loud or in your head.
contraindications: none significant

strategies.name: box breathing
strategies.instructions: Inhale for 4 counts. Hold for 4 counts. Exhale for 4 counts. Hold for 4 counts. Repeat 4-8 times.
contraindications: may increase anxiety in some with trauma history

strategies.name: progressive muscle relaxation
strategies.instructions: Tense each muscle group for 5 seconds, then release. Work from toes to head. Notice the contrast between tension and relaxation.
contraindications: muscle injuries
chronic pain conditions without adaptation

strategies.name: thought record
strategies.instructions: Write down the situation, automatic thought, emotion, evidence for and against the thought, and a balanced alternative thought.
contraindications: may feel overwhelming in acute crisis

strategies.name: behavioral activation scheduling
strategies.instructions: Schedule small activities that provide accomplishment or pleasure. Start very small (5-10 min). Rate expected vs actual mood change. Gradually increase.
contraindications: immediate crisis
severe fatigue from medical causes

strategies.name: self-compassion break
strategies.instructions: Acknowledge suffering ('this is hard'), recognize common humanity ('I'm not alone in this'), offer self-kindness ('may I be kind to myself').
contraindications: may feel uncomfortable initially for those with deep shame

strategies.name: worry time scheduling
strategies.instructions: Set aside 15 minutes at the same time daily to worry. At other times, postpone worries to worry time. During worry time, write out all concerns.
contraindications: may not work for generalized anxiety without professional guidance

strategies.name: gratitude journaling
strategies.instructions: Write 3 things you're grateful for daily. Be specific. Can include small things (a warm cup of tea). Focus on different things each day.
contraindications: may feel forced in severe depression

strategies.name: safe place visualization
strategies.instructions: Close your eyes. Imagine a place where you feel completely safe. Engage all senses — what do you see, hear, smell, feel? Stay here for several breaths.
contraindications: may be triggering for some trauma survivors
dissociation risk

strategies.name: TIPP skills (DBT)
strategies.instructions: Temperature (cold water on face), Intense exercise (jumping jacks), Paced breathing, Progressive muscle relaxation. Use during emotional crisis.
contraindications: cold water not safe for some heart conditions

strategies.name: cognitive defusion
strategies.instructions: Notice the thought ('I notice I'm having the thought that I'm a failure'). Add 'I notice' prefix. Say it in a silly voice. Imagine it on a leaf floating by.
contraindications: may feel dismissive if not introduced properly

strategies.name: opposite action
strategies.instructions: Identify the urge (withdraw, avoid, attack). Do the opposite (reach out, approach, gentle disengage). Notice how the emotion shifts.
best_situations: when acting on the urge would make things worse
contraindications: not appropriate when safety is at risk

strategies.name: mindful walking
strategies.instructions: Walk slowly. Notice each step. Feel the ground. Notice your surroundings with all senses. Return attention to walking when mind wanders.
contraindications: mobility limitations
unsafe walking environment

strategies.name: values-based action
strategies.instructions: Identify what matters to you. Choose one small action aligned with that value. Do it with attention and intention. Repeat daily.
contraindications: not suitable during acute crisis

strategies.name: sleep hygiene protocol
strategies.instructions: Consistent wake time. No screens 1 hour before bed. Cool dark room. No caffeine after 2pm. Wind-down routine. Get up if can't sleep for 20 min.
contraindications: may need professional help for severe insomnia

strategies.name: anger journaling
strategies.instructions: Write out what happened, what you felt, the intensity (1-10), the trigger, and what you needed. Do not censor. Write and then review later if helpful.
contraindications: rumination risk if not structured

strategies.name: social connection scheduling
strategies.instructions: Schedule one low-pressure social interaction per week. Could be coffee with a friend, joining a group, or a phone call. Start with safe, comfortable people.
contraindications: social anxiety may need graded approach

strategies.name: grounding through senses
strategies.instructions: Hold an ice cube. Smell a strong scent (peppermint, citrus). Touch different textures. Eat something with strong flavor. Use strong sensations to return to present.
contraindications: avoid pain-based grounding for self-harm urges

strategies.name: thought stopping technique
strategies.instructions: Notice the rumination. Say (out loud or in head) 'STOP.' Redirect to a pre-chosen neutral or positive thought. Practice repeatedly.
contraindications: may not work for OCD without professional guidance
suppression may worsen for some

strategies.name: 4-7-8 breathing
strategies.instructions: Inhale quietly through the nose for 4 seconds. Hold breath for 7 seconds. Exhale completely through the mouth for 8 seconds. Repeat 4 times.
contraindications: may be difficult with respiratory issues

strategies.name: worry tree
strategies.instructions: When worry arises, ask: Is this problem solvable? If no, practice acceptance. If yes, list possible solutions, choose one, and take action. Schedule follow-up.
contraindications: may not address underlying anxiety disorders

strategies.name: exposure hierarchy
strategies.instructions: List feared situations from least to most anxiety-provoking. Gradually expose yourself to each level while practicing coping skills. Rate anxiety before and after.
contraindications: should be done with professional guidance for severe anxiety

strategies.name: time blocking
strategies.instructions: Divide day into blocks for specific tasks. Include breaks. Prioritize high-energy times for difficult tasks. Protect blocks from interruptions.
contraindications: may feel rigid for some

strategies.name: boundary setting
strategies.instructions: Identify where you feel drained or resentful. Practice saying 'no' or setting limits. Use clear, kind language. Start with small boundaries.
contraindications: may cause conflict initially

strategies.name: digital detox
strategies.instructions: Set specific times to check devices. Turn off notifications. Create tech-free zones. Gradually increase detox periods.
contraindications: may not be feasible for work requirements

strategies.name: stress container
strategies.instructions: Imagine a container that holds your stress. Visualize what it looks like. Identify what's in it (worries, tasks). Decide what can be emptied now, later, or never.
contraindications: may be abstract for some

strategies.name: behavioral experiment
strategies.instructions: Identify a negative belief (e.g., 'I'm incompetent'). Design an experiment to test it. Predict outcome. Try the behavior. Record actual outcome. Compare.
contraindications: requires professional guidance for severe cases

strategies.name: connection planning
strategies.instructions: Make a list of people you can reach out to. Categorize by support type (emotional, practical). Schedule regular check-ins. Start with low-stakes contacts.
contraindications: if social anxiety is high, use graded exposure

strategies.name: STOP skill
strategies.instructions: Stop what you're doing. Take a breath. Observe your thoughts and feelings. Proceed mindfully. Use before responding in anger.
contraindications: may be hard in intense anger

strategies.name: anger thermometer
strategies.instructions: Rate anger from 0 (calm) to 10 (furious). Notice physical cues at each level. Develop coping strategies for different levels. Use to track patterns.
contraindications: none significant

strategies.name: assertive communication
strategies.instructions: Use 'I' statements: 'I feel [emotion] when [behavior] because [reason]. I would like [request].' Be clear, direct, and respectful. Practice in low-stakes situations first.
contraindications: may be misinterpreted in high-conflict relationships

strategies.name: continuing bonds
strategies.instructions: Find ways to maintain a connection with the deceased (talk to them, write letters, visit meaningful places). Focus on positive memories and ongoing influence.
contraindications: may delay acceptance if used to avoid grief

strategies.name: memory box
strategies.instructions: Create a physical or digital collection of items that remind you of the person or thing lost (photos, letters, objects). Visit when missing them.
contraindications: may increase sadness if used excessively

strategies.name: grief journaling
strategies.instructions: Write about your feelings, memories, and thoughts related to the loss. No structure needed. Date entries. Review periodically to notice changes.
contraindications: may be overwhelming in acute grief

strategies.name: recovery phases
strategies.instructions: Recognize stages: 1) Crisis (immediate relief), 2) Restoration (building new habits), 3) Renewal (redefining priorities). Progress gradually.
contraindications: requires patience and often professional support

strategies.name: energy audit
strategies.instructions: Track activities for a week, rating each as energy-giving or draining. Identify patterns. Adjust schedule to increase energizers and reduce drainers.
contraindications: may reveal difficult changes needed

strategies.name: micro-recovery
strategies.instructions: Take short breaks throughout the day (stretch, breathe, step outside). Use Pomodoro technique (25 min work, 5 min break). Prioritize recovery moments.
contraindications: may feel insufficient in severe burnout

strategies.name: social connection ladder
strategies.instructions: Gradually increase social exposure: 1) Be around people (café), 2) Brief interactions (cashier), 3) Low-stakes conversations, 4) Meaningful connections. Step up when ready.
contraindications: may be too slow for acute loneliness

strategies.name: interest-based groups
strategies.instructions: Join groups based on hobbies or interests (book clubs, sports teams, volunteer groups). Shared activity reduces social pressure. Commit to attending regularly.
contraindications: requires initial courage to join

strategies.name: DEAR MAN
strategies.instructions: Describe facts, Express feelings, Assert wishes, Reinforce positively. Be Mindful, Appear confident, Negotiate. Use for difficult conversations.
contraindications: requires practice and emotional regulation

strategies.name: active listening
strategies.instructions: Focus fully on speaker. Paraphrase what you heard. Validate feelings ('I understand why you feel that'). Ask clarifying questions. Avoid interrupting.
contraindications: may be difficult when emotionally triggered

strategies.name: Gottman repair
strategies.instructions: During conflict, make repair attempts: humor, affection, apology, meta-communication ('Can we start over?'). Accept partner's repair attempts.
contraindications: may not work in abusive dynamics

strategies.name: study wellness
strategies.instructions: Balance study with self-care: scheduled breaks, sleep, exercise, social time. Use active learning techniques. Seek help early. Maintain perspective.
contraindications: may require institutional support

strategies.name: exam anxiety toolkit
strategies.instructions: Prepare physically (sleep, nutrition), mentally (practice tests, positive self-talk), logistically (prepare materials). Use grounding during exam. Post-exam reflection.
contraindications: may need professional help for severe test anxiety

strategies.name: relaxation response
strategies.instructions: Sit quietly. Focus on a word or phrase. Breathe slowly. Allow body to relax. Practice daily for cumulative effect.
contraindications: may be difficult for highly anxious individuals initially

strategies.name: mindfulness meditation
strategies.instructions: Sit comfortably. Focus on breath. Notice thoughts without judgment. Return attention to breath. Start with 5 minutes daily, increase gradually.
contraindications: may increase anxiety in some trauma survivors

strategies.name: mood tracking
strategies.instructions: Rate mood daily (1-10). Note activities, sleep, interactions. Look for patterns over time. Use apps or journals.
contraindications: may become obsessive for some

strategies.name: anger iceberg
strategies.instructions: When angry, ask: What's beneath the anger? Often anger masks hurt, fear, or shame. Identify the primary emotion. Address that instead.
contraindications: may require therapeutic exploration

strategies.name: relationship love maps
strategies.instructions: Regularly update knowledge of partner's world: stresses, hopes, preferences. Ask open-ended questions. Show genuine interest. Build emotional bank account.
contraindications: requires mutual effort
